title_en: Al-Istidhkar: Ibn Abd al-Barr's Juristic Companion
title_ar: الاستذكار: الرفيق الفقهي لابن عبد البر
content_en:
Al-Istidhkar al-Jami li-Madhahib Fuqaha al-Amsar — The Comprehensive Memoir of the Legal Positions of the Jurists of the Major Cities — is the second of Ibn Abd al-Barr's two great commentaries on the Muwatta of Imam Malik, and it serves as a perfect complement to the encyclopedic At-Tamhid. Where At-Tamhid is organized by the names of the narrators in Imam Malik's chains and extends to extraordinary length, Al-Istidhkar is organized by topic in the conventional sequence of Islamic jurisprudence and aims at systematic completeness in its presentation of scholarly positions rather than exhaustive chain analysis.

Abu Umar ibn Abd al-Barr composed Al-Istidhkar as a topically organized digest of the scholars' positions on the legal questions raised by each hadith of the Muwatta. The title Istidhkar relates to the concept of preserving or memorizing scholarly knowledge — the work aims to gather and preserve the positions of the major scholars on every question the Muwatta addresses, creating a comprehensive reference for the comparative legal implications of the foundational Maliki text.

The two works — At-Tamhid and Al-Istidhkar — are designed to be used together, and Ibn Abd al-Barr himself indicated that they were conceived as a pair. At-Tamhid provides the deep chain analysis and biographical context for each tradition, while Al-Istidhkar provides the topically organized legal analysis. Scholars who need to understand the hadith basis of a legal ruling would turn to At-Tamhid; those who need to understand the range of scholarly positions on that ruling would turn to Al-Istidhkar. Together they constitute the most complete engagement with the Muwatta in the classical tradition.

Modern editions of Al-Istidhkar, running to twenty-seven volumes, have made this landmark work more accessible than it was in manuscript form, and digital editions with searchable text have further enhanced its utility for contemporary researchers. It remains, alongside At-Tamhid, one of the most important resources in the hadith and legal sciences produced by Andalusian scholarship.
